evals.prompt: Add MatrixScan Pick to my app. The user will provide the real product list and symbologies later — go with a small placeholder product list and a reasonable default symbology set. File: NonScanditViewController.swift
evals.expected_output: The skill adds the MatrixScan Pick pipeline to the existing ScanViewController: imports ScanditBarcodeCapture, creates the DataCaptureContext, builds BarcodePickSettings with a default symbology set, builds a set of BarcodePickProduct, creates a BarcodePickAsyncMapperProductProvider with a provider delegate that maps scanned payloads to product identifiers, constructs the BarcodePick mode, creates a BarcodePickView and adds it to the view hierarchy, wires the BarcodePickViewUIDelegate finish handler, and drives the start()/pause()/stop() lifecycle. The response also shows the setup checklist (SPM, Info.plist, license key).

assertions.text: import ScanditBarcodeCapture is present
A DataCaptureContext is created with DataCaptureContext(licenseKey:) using the placeholder '-- ENTER YOUR SCANDIT LICENSE KEY HERE --'
BarcodePickSettings is constructed and at least two symbologies are enabled via settings.set(symbology:enabled:)
A set of BarcodePickProduct is built, each constructed with an identifier and a quantityToPick
A BarcodePickAsyncMapperProductProvider is constructed with the products and a providerDelegate

The view controller conforms to BarcodePickAsyncMapperProductProviderDelegate and implements mapItems(_:completionHandler:) returning BarcodePickProductProviderCallbackItem values built with itemData and productIdentifier
The BarcodePick mode is constructed via BarcodePick(context:settings:productProvider:)

A BarcodePickView is constructed with BarcodePickView(frame:context:barcodePick:settings:) (a BarcodePickViewSettings instance) and added to the hierarchy with addSubview (the view does NOT add itself)
barcodePickView.start() is called in viewWillAppear
barcodePickView.pause() is called in viewWillDisappear, and stop() is called only when isMovingFromParent is true
The view controller conforms to BarcodePickViewUIDelegate and implements barcodePickViewDidTapFinishButton(_:); barcodePickView.uiDelegate is set

The view controller conforms to BarcodePickActionListener (implements didPickItem(withData:completionHandler:) and didUnpickItem(withData:completionHandler:), each calling completionHandler(true)) and registers it via barcodePickView.addActionListener(self) — required for picks to actually complete
The existing ScanViewController class is preserved (not renamed or removed); the titleLabel IBOutlet or viewDidLoad title assignment is preserved or the user is told it was kept
The response includes the setup checklist: SPM URL (https://github.com/Scandit/datacapture-spm), NSCameraUsageDescription in Info.plist, replace the license key placeholder
No custom BarcodePickViewHighlightStyleCustomView / highlightStyle customization is added (default highlights are fine for the basic integration)

evals.prompt: Add MatrixScan Pick to my SwiftUI app. File: NonScanditSwiftUIView.swift
evals.expected_output: The skill adds a UIKit view controller with the full MatrixScan Pick pipeline (context, BarcodePickSettings + symbologies, product provider, BarcodePick mode, BarcodePickView added with addSubview, lifecycle, finish-button UI delegate, and BarcodePickActionListener), wraps it in a UIViewControllerRepresentable, and changes the existing SwiftUI ScanView body to use that representable. No Scandit API calls live on the SwiftUI side.

assertions.text: import ScanditBarcodeCapture is present

A UIViewController subclass is added with the full MatrixScan Pick setup: DataCaptureContext, BarcodePickSettings with at least two symbologies enabled via set(symbology:enabled:), a set of BarcodePickProduct (identifier + quantityToPick), BarcodePickAsyncMapperProductProvider with a providerDelegate, BarcodePick(context:settings:productProvider:), BarcodePickView(frame:context:barcodePick:settings:) added with addSubview, and start()/pause()/stop() in viewWillAppear/viewWillDisappear (pause then stop only when isMovingFromParent)
The view controller conforms to BarcodePickAsyncMapperProductProviderDelegate and implements mapItems(_:completionHandler:) returning BarcodePickProductProviderCallbackItem values
The view controller conforms to BarcodePickViewUIDelegate and implements barcodePickViewDidTapFinishButton(_:); barcodePickView.uiDelegate is set

The view controller conforms to BarcodePickActionListener (didPickItem(withData:completionHandler:) and didUnpickItem(withData:completionHandler:), each calling completionHandler(true)) and registers it via barcodePickView.addActionListener(self) — required for picks to complete
A UIViewControllerRepresentable-conforming struct is added that makes/updates the UIKit scan view controller
The existing SwiftUI ScanView body uses the new representable (no longer the plain Text placeholder)
No Scandit API call (DataCaptureContext, BarcodePick, BarcodePickView, etc.) appears inside the SwiftUI View struct body
No Scandit API call appears inside makeUIViewController or updateUIViewController beyond constructing the UIKit view controller
The response includes the setup checklist: SPM URL (https://github.com/Scandit/datacapture-spm), NSCameraUsageDescription in Info.plist, replace the license key placeholder
